from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a comics character (or related entities)
    into a single fluent sentence.  Each known predicate is rendered with a
    natural‑language template; unknown predicates fall back to a simple
    “subject predicate object” wording.
    """
    # Full‑sentence templates (subject + object)
    full_templates = {
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "creator": "{subject} was created by {object}",
        "fullName": "{subject}'s full name is {object}",
        "foundedBy": "{subject} was founded by {object}",
        "keyPerson": "{object} is a key person at {subject}",
        "city": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "product": "{subject} produces {object}",
        "lastAired": "{subject} last aired on {object}",
        "starring": "{subject} stars {object}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": "{subject} first appeared in the film {object}",
        "voice": "{subject} is voiced by {object}",
        "distributor": "The film {subject} was distributed by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} is {object}",
        "award": "{subject} received the {object}",
        "broadcastedBy": "{subject} was broadcasted by {object}",
        "firstAired": "{subject} first aired on {object}",
        "series": "{subject} is part of the series {object}",
        "child": "{subject} has a child named {object}",
    }

    # Phrase‑only templates for grouping multiple facts about the same subject
    phrase_templates = {
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {object}",
        "creator": "was created by {object}",
        "fullName": "'s full name is {object}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {object}",
        "keyPerson": "{object} is a key person at {subject}",
        "city": "is located in {object}",
        "product": "produces {object}",
        "lastAired": "last aired on {object}",
        "starring": "stars {object}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": "first appeared in the film {object}",
        "voice": "is voiced by {object}",
        "distributor": "was distributed by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {object}",
        "nationality": "is {object}",
        "award": "received the {object}",
        "broadcastedBy": "was broadcasted by {object}",
        "firstAired": "first aired on {object}",
        "series": "is part of the series {object}",
        "child": "has a child named {object}",
    }

    # Helper to clean strings
    def clean(text: str) -> str:
        t = text.replace(" (comicsCharacter)", "").strip()
        if (t.startswith('"') and t.endswith('"')) or (t.startswith("'") and t.endswith("'")):
            t = t[1:-1]
        return t

    # Group triples by subject
    groups: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for tr in triples:
        subj = clean(tr.subject)
        groups.setdefault(subj, []).append(Triple(subj, tr.predicate, clean(tr.object)))

    sentences: list[str] = []
    for subj, group in groups.items():
        if len(group) == 1:
            tr = group[0]
            tmpl = full_templates.get(tr.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                clause = tmpl.format(subject=subj, object=tr.object)
            else:
                clause = f"{subj} {tr.predicate} {tr.object}"
            sentences.append(clause)
        else:
            # Build a compact clause with subject followed by phrases
            phrases: list[str] = []
            for tr in group:
                tmpl = phrase_templates.get(tr.predicate)
                if tmpl:
                    # Some phrase templates need the subject (e.g., keyPerson)
                    phrase = tmpl.format(subject=subj, object=tr.object)
                else:
                    # Fallback to full template then strip leading subject
                    full_tmpl = full_templates.get(tr.predicate, "{subject} " + tr.predicate + " {object}")
                    phrase = full_tmpl.format(subject=subj, object=tr.object)
                    if phrase.lower().startswith(subj.lower()):
                        phrase = phrase[len(subj):].lstrip()
                phrases.append(phrase)
            # Combine phrases with commas and an 'and' before the last one
            if len(phrases) == 2:
                combined = f"{subj} {phrases[0]} and {phrases[1]}"
            else:
                combined = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
            sentences.append(combined)

    if not sentences:
        return ""

    # Join different subject clauses into one sentence
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        final = sentences[0]
    else:
        final = ", ".join(sentences[:-1]) + ", and " + sentences[-1]

    # Capitalize first character and ensure a single period at the end
    final = final.strip()
    final = final[0].upper() + final[1:]
    if not final.endswith("."):
        final += "."
    return final

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
